snippets.title: Aaron Levie on coding and models
snippets.takeaway: The Cursor deal is symbolically quite significant. It was effectively the first mega success in the applied layer of AI.
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 2 recent updates. The Cursor deal is symbolically quite significant. It was effectively the first mega success in the applied layer of AI. They firmly proved out the value proposition of having a deep domain focus, the role you play as a model router, when to lean into frontier models vs. when to train your own, and the role of applied AI GTM and distribution to make sure you’re actually taking advantage of the market opportunity.

snippets.title: Zara Zhang on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: You don’t have to chase the cool thing. Do whatever you’re already doing so well that it becomes the cool thing. The cool thing is rarely cool when it starts.
snippets.summary: Zara Zhang (zarazhangrui on X) shared 2 recent updates. You don’t have to chase the cool thing. Do whatever you’re already doing so well that it becomes the cool thing. The cool thing is rarely cool when it starts. It becomes cool because someone went deep on something everyone else thought was too small. Every other product right now is "an AI agent that does everything in your work & life & integrates with everything." Cool, that's just Claude/Codex.

snippets.title: Building intelligent apps for Apple platforms with Claude in the Foundation Models framework
snippets.takeaway: Building intelligent apps for Apple platforms with Claude in the Foundation Models framework. Today we're releasing Foundation Models framework support for Claude through a...
snippets.summary: Building intelligent apps for Apple platforms with Claude in the Foundation Models framework. Today we're releasing Foundation Models framework support for Claude through a new Swift package that lets Apple developers use Apple's Foundation Models framework to call Claude for more complex workflows. Apple’s Foundation Models framework gives developers access to tap into models natively from Swift.

snippets.title: Simulating Humans at Scale: Simile's Joon Sung Park
snippets.takeaway: Simulating Humans at Scale: Simile's Joon Sung Park. We found that to be so interesting, and we found that to be the ingredient that we had been waiting for for creating really...
snippets.summary: We found that to be so interesting, and we found that to be the ingredient that we had been waiting for for creating really complex agentic behaviors. So Smallville was a project that we were running at Stanford, where the idea was that we made this observation that large language models can now encode a lot of human behavior that is embedded in its training data from the web and social media and so forth, that if you sort of prove at the right angle, you can actually get a lot of microbehaviors out of these...
